scope: SURFACE-ONLY (thread 3.2). The observed log: feasibility path already exists in rarity-core.ts; Chewgether's no-data is honest (0 logs). NO rarity-core.ts engine change in any story.

stories.title: Build the goal-generic FeasibilityReadout server component
stories.value: so that any goal's feasibility can be rendered honestly in the dashboard from a single GoalFeasibility, without an MCP round-trip and without re-hardcoding a vertical
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/components/FeasibilityReadout.tsx is a server component (no "use client" directive, no React hooks/state).

Component accepts an already-serialized GoalFeasibility prop; it never accepts or constructs a Date instance (GoalFeasibility.computedAt is already a string, weeksRemaining a number) — no Date crosses the server/client boundary.

Renders exactly four honest states selected purely from the GoalFeasibility value: (1) unratedReason==="someday" -> "No deadline set — feasibility unrated."; (2) unratedReason==="no-targets" -> "Add targets to rate feasibility."; (3) unratedReason==="no-data" (see two sub-states below); (4) tier set -> full readout with per-target requiredRate vs observedRate, weeksRemaining, verdict tier, and rateBasis.

The no-data state renders TWO distinct sub-states keyed on requiredRate===null: 0-log sub-state (requiredRate===null AND observedRate===null) -> copy can only say e.g. "Not enough logged data to rate yet — log the metric a few times" / "Add at least one entry to compute the pace you need" (it must NOT promise a per-week target, because requiredRate is null at zero logs per plan-critique C-1); 1–2-log sub-state (requiredRate populated, observedRate null) -> shows requiredRate (e.g. "needs ~$66/wk to hit $1,000 by 9/30") with observedRate rendered as "— not yet estimable".
Goal-generic: zero hardcoded references to fitness, Elbert, Chewgether, MRR, or any vertical-specific label — all labels derive from the GoalFeasibility perTarget entries.
Any date label (e.g. target date) is formatted via @/lib/calendar USER_TZ helpers, never a raw new Date()/toLocaleString without timeZone.
NO change to src/lib/rarity-core.ts: git diff --stat shows src/lib/rarity-core.ts is untouched by this story.

Chewgether criterion: rendered against the live Chewgether GoalFeasibility (0 logs) the component shows 'not enough logged data to rate' honestly today; rendered against a >=3-MRR-log fixture GoalFeasibility it shows a real verdict (tier + rateBasis "observed").
npx tsc --noEmit and npm run lint pass.

stories.title: Surface FeasibilityReadout on Today (fitness hero + ProjectTodayView)
stories.value: so that the user sees an honest feasibility readout on the Today page for both fitness and project focus goals, computed server-side from the same function the coach's get_goal reads
acceptanceCriteria: src/app/page.tsx fitness hero renders <FeasibilityReadout> with feasibility resolved server-side via computeGoalFeasibility(focusGoal) (the same fn get_goal calls) — NOT via an MCP round-trip.

src/components/ProjectTodayView.tsx renders <FeasibilityReadout> (placed between the MRR card and the next-milestone card), resolving feasibility server-side from its already-fetched targets + goal.targetDate; ProjectTodayView stays a server component (no "use client").
No Date instance is passed into FeasibilityReadout or any client child; feasibility is passed already-serialized.

Chewgether criterion: with a project focus goal, Today shows the honest no-data readout ('not enough logged data to rate') today; with the fitness focus goal, Today shows that goal's feasibility readout. Fitness Today is otherwise visually unchanged.
NO change to src/lib/rarity-core.ts (git diff --stat confirms).
npx tsc --noEmit, npm run lint, and npm run build pass.
dependsOn: Build the goal-generic FeasibilityReadout server component

stories.title: Surface FeasibilityReadout on the goal detail page
stories.value: so that opening any goal shows its honest feasibility readout, consistent with Today and the coach's get_goal view
acceptanceCriteria: src/app/goals/[id]/page.tsx renders <FeasibilityReadout> for the viewed goal, with feasibility resolved server-side via computeGoalFeasibility(goal) (no MCP round-trip).
Works for both a fitness goal (real readout) and the Chewgether project goal (honest no-data sub-state today).
No Date instance is passed to FeasibilityReadout; feasibility is passed already-serialized; any date label uses @/lib/calendar USER_TZ.
NO change to src/lib/rarity-core.ts (git diff --stat confirms).
npx tsc --noEmit and npm run lint pass.
dependsOn: Build the goal-generic FeasibilityReadout server component

stories.title: Advertise feasibility in get_goal's description and update the coaching doc
stories.value: so that the coach discovers (from the tool description) that get_goal already carries per-target feasibility, and the coaching prompt explains the readout + that logging MRR unlocks the rate estimate
acceptanceCriteria: The get_goal tool *description* in src/lib/mcp/tools.ts gains one sentence advertising feasibility.computed — per-target requiredRate/observedRate/verdict, weeksRemaining, and unratedReason (someday|no-targets|no-data).
Only the description string changes for get_goal: its returned shape, handler logic, and computeGoalFeasibility are untouched.

docs/coaching/project-goal-prompts.md is updated to mention the FeasibilityReadout surface (Today + goal page) and that logging MRR >=3 times unlocks the observed-rate estimate (no-data is honest until then).
MCP curl tools/list shows the updated get_goal description and the same total tool count/names as before.
NO change to src/lib/rarity-core.ts (git diff --stat confirms).
npx tsc --noEmit and npm run lint pass.

stories.title: Verify the feasibility surface end-to-end across both verticals with no engine change
stories.value: so that we prove Chewgether reads honestly today, a >=3-MRR-log fixture flips to a real verdict, fitness is un-regressed, and rarity-core.ts was never touched
acceptanceCriteria: npx tsc --noEmit, npm run lint, and npm run build all pass on the integrated sprint.
MCP curl smoke (dev server running): get_goal on Chewgether returns feasibility.computed with unratedReason "no-data" (unchanged honest behavior) and the updated tool description.

Browser smoke at <=390px: Chewgether Today + goal detail page show 'not enough logged data to rate' honestly; the fitness goal's Today + goal detail page show its feasibility readout; fitness Today is visually unchanged.

Chewgether criterion proven: inserting a synthetic >=3-row MRR LogEntry fixture for Chewgether makes FeasibilityReadout render a real verdict (tier + rateBasis "observed"); the fixture is removed afterward and the surface returns to the honest no-data sub-state.
Both no-data sub-states are observed: 0-log (requiredRate null) and a 1–2-log fixture (requiredRate populated, observedRate '— not yet estimable').
Final guardrail: git diff against the sprint base shows NO change to src/lib/rarity-core.ts anywhere in Sprint 8.
dependsOn: Build the goal-generic FeasibilityReadout server component
Surface FeasibilityReadout on Today (fitness hero + ProjectTodayView)
Surface FeasibilityReadout on the goal detail page
Advertise feasibility in get_goal's description and update the coaching doc
